shape_semantics: I2S-ANTI. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,W,_,W: cmplog BLOCKS, while value_profile AND naive RESOLVE (vpc non-decisive). The technique that HURTS is I2S (cmplog's input-to-state substitution): the branch is reached by plain havoc byte/token exploration (naive) and by the gradient (value_profile), but cmplog's extra I2S stage diverts or pollutes the corpus and BLOCKS it. The shape (Layer 1) already asserts I2S-anti (cmplog is the sole loser). The evidence test does NOT re-confirm that cmplog blocks (that would verify the loser-pattern with the loser-pattern, violating G2); it independently discriminates WHY I2S hurts on each branch -- whether the I2S stage (a) actively splices a WRONG/competing operand that DEPLETES the cmplog corpus of the target byte region at a fixed offset, or (b) has NO harvestable input-resident operand at all (the gate value is table/state/property-derived) so I2S is pure overhead, or (c) the gate is a length/sequence-accumulation gate that cmplog's CMP-biased budget SHORTENS the input away from, or (d) the gate fires on iteration/traversal DEPTH so the winner corpus drives the branch line DEEPER per seed, or (e) the comparison operand is a DECODED codepoint reachable in raw input byte-space (winner lands the byte-pair, loser stalls), or (f) -- the NEW round-2 mechanism -- I2S HOMOGENIZES the whole cmplog corpus onto a few CMP-matched byte motifs (a target-wide corpus-composition signature, branch-independent), collapsing the byte-diversity that the winner's structural/state-machine gates need, on gates where no per-branch seed-set tool can get data.

hypotheses.label: I2S splices a WRONG/competing but abundant CMP operand into the input (a well-known font-table tag, a script-tag/codepoint constant, an in-range fixed-point magnitude, or a competing protocol value), pinning the cmplog corpus onto that decoy and DEPLETING it of the target byte value at the winner gate's offset; naive/value_profile never harvest the decoy so they freely reach the target

params.comment: For the branch's W/L gate offset (the divergent byte from the analysis byte-diff / per-role overlay), compute the cmplog-vs-naive log2 enrichment of the TARGET byte value (the winner-resolving value at that offset) vs the DECOY value (the competing operand I2S substitutes). Anti-direction: cmplog DEPLETES the target (signed_target_enrich < 0) and ENRICHES the decoy (decoy_enrich > 0).
params.target_value_source: signature.operand_literal (e.g. DFontTag 00 00 01 00 for 5391; PNG_FIXED_ERROR overflow byte for libpng/3977; 0x3a for openthread/13349,13391; table-tag constants for 5815/5843/5857) at the cmp-site offset from the per-role overlay

hypotheses.prediction: On the gate offset the cmplog corpus is DEPLETED of the target value relative to naive (signed_target_enrich < -0.3) AND/OR ENRICHED in the decoy operand (decoy_enrich > 0): I2S is actively steering bytes AWAY from the winner value. A pure-overhead mechanism (I2S has no operand to substitute) would predict signed_target_enrich ~ 0 and operand_enrichment returning no_gate_signature -- no directional depletion, because there is no decoy to splice. A pro-I2S mechanism would predict signed_target_enrich > 0. Only the active-decoy-substitution mechanism predicts a NEGATIVE target enrichment / positive decoy enrichment, which is what distinguishes 'I2S splices the wrong abundant operand' from 'I2S simply has nothing to do here'.

hypotheses.label: The winner gate needs havoc to BUILD or PRESERVE a long / periodically-repeated glyph-record byte sequence (multi-matra record runs, long multi-glyph Thai state-machine traversal); the gate value is table/state-derived (no harvestable operand), but cmplog's CMP-biased budget produces SHORTER, heterogeneous inputs that never reach the required sequence length, while naive's byte-expansion/copy operators grow and sustain it

params.comment: Reuse joint_necessity's struct-size half: take the median seed SIZE of the winner-resolving (naive/value_profile) seed set vs the cmplog-BLOCKING seed set. size_lift = median(resolving_size) / median(blocking_size). The length/accumulation subtype predicts the resolving seeds are materially LONGER than the blocking seeds. --tokens is unused for this hypothesis (size only); the token-count half of the tool is ignored here.

tokens_by_target.harfbuzz: (size-only; token vocabulary not used for this hypothesis)

hypotheses.prediction: The winner-resolving seeds are materially LONGER than the cmplog-blocking seeds (size_lift >= 1.3): the divergence is driven by sequence/length accumulation that cmplog's CMP-biased, shorter inputs fail to build. A no-operand-overhead mechanism (subtype 3 below) would predict size_lift ~ 1.0 -- cmplog and naive reach SIMILARLY-sized inputs and the gate is simply a derived category cmplog can't aim at, with no length asymmetry. A decoy-substitution mechanism would have a fixed-offset operand signature (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature). This length asymmetry, on a gate with NO harvestable operand, is what separates 'I2S shortens the input below the accumulation threshold' from plain 'I2S overhead on a derived gate'.

hypotheses.label: The gate value is computed from shaping tables, Unicode-property lookups, Ragel state-machine internals, or derived category classification -- NOT a literal value compared against input bytes anywhere. I2S has no input-resident CMP operand to harvest or substitute; its per-execution callback overhead and misdirected substitution budget crowd out the broad havoc byte exploration that resolves the branch, with NO directional corpus depletion and NO length asymmetry

params.comment: operand_enrichment is asked for a gate signature; on these branches it returns no_gate_signature because the gate is NOT a fixed-offset value comparison (it is a derived/table/state category). The DIAGNOSTIC for this hypothesis is the ABSENCE of a harvestable operand (skip == no_gate_signature) COMBINED WITH the absence of a length asymmetry (the i2s_disrupts_havoc_sequence_accumulation hypothesis, tried first, did NOT fire). This is the residual / catch-all I2S-anti subtype.

hypotheses.prediction: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (no fixed-offset operand to harvest) AND there is no resolving-vs-blocking length asymmetry (size_lift < 1.3, so the sequence-accumulation hypothesis is ruled out): I2S simply has nothing to substitute and is pure overhead. A decoy-substitution mechanism would instead yield a fixed-offset operand with negative target enrichment (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature, signed_target_enrich < 0). A sequence-accumulation mechanism would yield size_lift >= 1.3. The conjunction no_gate_signature AND size_lift<1.3 is exactly what isolates 'derived gate, no operand, no length effect -- just I2S overhead' from the two operand/length subtypes.

hypotheses.label: The winner gate is a loop-exit guard or a deep Ragel state-machine action whose firing depends on how DEEP the input drives the per-buffer traversal (delete_glyphs_inplace backward-cluster loop reaching its false exit; find_syllables_myanmar walking the state machine into a late case action). There is no input-resident CMP operand (operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature) and no whole-input length asymmetry (size_lift < 1.3, so the sequence-accumulation hypothesis does NOT fire); the distinguishing signal is per-seed iteration DEPTH at the branch line: the winner (naive/value_profile) corpus drives the branch line DEEPER per seed, while cmplog's CMP-biased budget reaches it far fewer times. I2S overhead here is paid as fewer/shallower traversals, not as a competing operand or a shorter input.

params.comment: Run the winner-resolving vs loser-blocking seed sets through the libafl-harfbuzz-cov image and read the per-seed-normalized execution count of the branch line. depth_lift = winner_depth / loser_depth. The iteration-depth subtype predicts the winner drives the branch line materially deeper per seed. NOTE (round-2): the two round-1 hint members (5311, 5931) did NOT clear this rule in the campaign (depth_lift < 1.3 or unscorable for lack of >=3 seeds), so they were REASSIGNED in round-2 to the new whole-corpus homogenization hypothesis (i2s_homogenizes_cmplog_corpus). This hypothesis is KEPT verbatim (confirmed for >=1 branch elsewhere) but no longer hints those two.

hypotheses.prediction: The winner corpus drives the branch line DEEPER per seed (winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.3): the divergence is iteration/traversal depth that cmplog's CMP-substitution budget under-drives, NOT a competing operand (operand_enrichment returned no_gate_signature) and NOT a whole-input length effect (size_lift < 1.3). A pure no-operand-overhead gate predicts depth_lift ~ 1.0. A sequence-accumulation gate would have shown its asymmetry as raw size (size_lift >= 1.3) rather than per-seed depth. This per-seed depth advantage, on a gate with no operand and no length lift, is what separates 'cmplog under-drives the traversal' from 'cmplog just has nothing to substitute'.
hypotheses.rule: skip == 'no_gate_signature' AND size_lift < 1.3 AND winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.3

hypotheses.label: The gate is an equality on a value DECODED inside the shaper (a UTF/codepoint slot compared to a small set of constants, e.g. Thai consonants 0x0E1B/0x0E1D/0x0E1F), so I2S harvests the comparison operand but rewrites the WRONG input offsets (the decoded codepoint does not sit at the contiguous input field I2S logs); yet the target value IS reachable in raw input byte-space as a short byte-pair the winner (naive/value_profile) lands by undirected mutation while the loser stalls. Distinct from decoy-substitution because operand_enrichment finds NO directional fixed-offset depletion (the byte does not live at a stable corpus offset), and distinct from pure derived-gate overhead because the operand value IS approachable in byte-space.

params.comment: operand value = the decoded codepoint byte-pair(s) from signature.operand_literal as little-endian byte-pairs (width 2). Slide a 2-byte window over each arm's seeds; per-arm min Hamming distance to the nearest matching byte-pair. Winner (naive/vp) lands the pair; loser (cmplog) stalls. NOTE (round-2): the round-1 hint member harfbuzz/5954 (Thai 0x0E1B/D/F) did NOT clear this rule in the campaign -- the width-2 operand is chance-alignment prone and cmplog ALSO lands 0x0E-high byte-pairs (just a non-matching low byte), so winner_closer was false / distance_gap < 0.15. With no built tool able to separate winner from loser on a width-2 decoded operand, 5954 was REASSIGNED in round-2 to the decidable:false group (i2s_oversolve_postshaping_derived_predicate). This hypothesis is KEPT verbatim (confirmed for >=1 branch elsewhere) but no longer hints 5954.
params.width_caveat: operand width 2 is chance-alignment prone (registry caveat); require a non-trivial distance_gap (>= 0.15) so a coincidental near-match does not pass.

hypotheses.prediction: The winner arm lands materially CLOSER to the operand byte-pair than the loser (winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.15): the decoded codepoint IS reachable in raw byte-space and the winner's undirected mutation lands it while cmplog's I2S-substituted wrong offsets stall. A decoy-substitution mechanism would instead show a fixed-offset corpus depletion (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature with signed_target_enrich < -0.3). A pure derived-gate-overhead mechanism (no byte-reachable operand at all) would predict winner_closer == false / distance_gap ~ 0. The winner's byte-space proximity advantage to a DECODED operand is exactly what isolates this from both neighbours.

hypotheses.label: I2S COLLAPSES the cmplog corpus's byte-diversity onto a few CMP-matched byte motifs (runs of a magic constant, repeated font-table-tag ASCII fragments like 'hasc'/'csah'/'SHOT', a self-reinforcing low-byte lexical stride like '72 10 00'/'8d 10 00'), so the WHOLE cmplog corpus is BOTH inflated with near-duplicate substitution products AND homogenized in byte composition. The winner gates here are structural / Ragel state-machine actions that require DIVERSE, freely-varied glyph-category byte runs (Myanmar / Khmer category sequences, backward-cluster loop volume); cmplog's homogenized corpus never produces that diversity while naive's undirected havoc does. Measured at the WHOLE-CORPUS level (branch-independent) because these gates have no fixed-offset operand, no byte-reachable literal, no per-seed length or depth asymmetry that any per-branch seed-set tool could get data for -- the signal lives only in cmplog-vs-naive corpus-composition.

params.comment: Whole-corpus, BRANCH-INDEPENDENT comparison of the cmplog saved corpus vs the naive saved corpus for this target (memoized per (target, arm-pair) by the arbiter). winner-fuzzer = cmplog (the arm hypothesized to inflate AND homogenize), loser-fuzzer = naive (the flat baseline). This is the registry's i2s_homogenizes_corpus rule (see corpus_size_ratio.py docstring): corpus_size_ratio >= 1.3 (cmplog hoards near-duplicate substitution products) AND composition_entropy_ratio < 0.85 (cmplog byte-composition collapsed onto few motifs).
params.ORCHESTRATOR_WIRING_REQUIRED: bench/arbitrate.py drives corpus_size_ratio's winner/loser arms ONLY from CS_ARMS.get(shape); shape 'i2s_vp_LW_W' is NOT in CS_ARMS yet, so _cs_winner/_cs_loser are None and the tool returns unscorable. To make this hypothesis scorable the orchestrator must add  "i2s_vp_LW_W": ("cmplog", "naive")  to CS_ARMS in bench/arbitrate.py (analogous to the existing ctx_coverage_LW and i2s_vp_LWLW entries). No new tool code is needed -- corpus_size_ratio is already built and validated for harfbuzz; this is a one-line shape->arm-pair registration.
params.target_realities: corpus_size_ratio needs on-disk corpora for BOTH arms; on s4 that covers harfbuzz/openthread (all 5 harfbuzz hint members live there). It is branch-independent, so a single harfbuzz cmplog-vs-naive scan scores every harfbuzz branch routed here -- this hypothesis therefore confirms a SHARED target-level homogenization mechanism for the group, it does not claim to discriminate them from each other.

hypotheses.prediction: The cmplog corpus is BOTH inflated and homogenized relative to naive (corpus_size_ratio >= 1.3 AND composition_entropy_ratio < 0.85): I2S substitution products hoard near-duplicate seeds and collapse byte-diversity, starving the diverse glyph-category runs the winner's state-machine gates need. A 'no corpus effect' alternative (cmplog and naive corpora are similarly sized and similarly diverse, so I2S is merely passive per-execution overhead -- the existing i2s_overhead_on_derived_gate_no_operand story) predicts corpus_size_ratio ~ 1.0 AND composition_entropy_ratio ~ 1.0 and would NOT fire this rule. A fixed-offset decoy-substitution mechanism would have shown a per-offset depletion (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature). This whole-corpus inflation+entropy-collapse signature, on gates with no fixed-offset operand and no per-seed size/depth asymmetry, is what separates an ACTIVE corpus-homogenization mechanism (I2S poisons the corpus globally) from PASSIVE per-execution overhead, which is the discriminator the per-branch seed-set tools could not get data for on these branches.

hypotheses.label: (decidable:false) The gate is a post-shaping / decoded-property predicate whose effect is I2S OVER-SOLVING the comparison -- cmplog harvests the decoded operand and over-commits the corpus to the TRUE side, starving the winner's FALSE-side reach (so cmplog reaches MORE of a specific structure, not less): a multi-codepoint conjunct where cmplog satisfies ZWJ-followed-by-Extended_Pictographic (5723), a post-shaping script enum where cmplog keeps producing Malayalam/Tamil (5881), or a width-2 decoded Thai codepoint where cmplog lands a 0x0E-high byte-pair with the wrong low byte (5954). No BUILT measurement can confirm the over-solve story: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (no fixed input offset); value_distance_reached has no usable byte-reachable literal for the script/conjunct cases and FAILED on the width-2 Thai operand (cmplog lands 0x0E-pairs too, distance_gap < 0.15); there is no length asymmetry; the predicate fires at a fixed buffer prefix so depth_reach shows no per-seed depth advantage; and corpus_size_ratio's homogenization signal MISPREDICTS here, because these losers reach a SPECIFIC over-committed structure (true conjunct / a particular script) rather than being starved of diversity. It is therefore indistinguishable from generic I2S overhead with current tools.

params.comment: These branches compare DECODED/post-shaping values (UTF-decoded codepoints, table-derived script enum, decoded conjuncts) that have no stable input offset and no byte-reachable literal usable by value_distance_reached. The over-solve direction (cmplog reaches the TRUE side / a SPECIFIC script) is the opposite of corpus-homogenization-starvation, so corpus_size_ratio cannot stand in: its entropy-collapse signature would be a FALSE positive here (these losers are directed, not starved). No built tool can separate the over-solve story from generic I2S overhead, so they are honestly left non-discriminable. round-2: 5934 MOVED OUT of this group to i2s_homogenizes_cmplog_corpus (its loser corpus IS homogenized onto a repeating '72 10 00' stride -- a starvation, not an over-solve, signature); 5954 MOVED IN (its width-2 value_distance test failed and no other tool discriminates).

hypotheses.prediction: No built measurement discriminates the over-solve mechanism from generic I2S overhead here: operand_enrichment == no_gate_signature (no fixed-offset operand); value_distance_reached has no usable byte-reachable literal (script enums / decoded conjuncts) or fails the width-2 chance-alignment floor (5954, distance_gap < 0.15); size_lift < 1.3 (no length asymmetry); depth_lift ~ 1.0 (predicate fires at a fixed buffer prefix); and corpus_size_ratio would FALSE-positive (these losers over-solve toward a specific structure rather than being homogenized away from diversity). Honest decidable:false: do NOT force a label.

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage 'I2S event' is used anywhere -- I2S is untagged in mutation_op. The decoy signature is read from cmplog CORPUS byte frequencies (operand_enrichment); the depth signal from per-seed branch-line execution counts (depth_reach); the value-distance signal from seed byte windows (value_distance_reached); and the NEW homogenization signal from whole-corpus byte composition (corpus_size_ratio) -- never from a lineage gradient/I2S event.

value_profile is treated as a feedback, not a mutator: its effect is read only via what the resolving corpus REACHES (size, byte-distance, branch-line depth) or via cmplog-vs-naive corpus composition, never via a lineage event.

byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is used ONLY for the fixed-offset VALUE-gate subtype (decoy-substitution), where W and L seeds align at the gate offset. The sequence-accumulation subtype uses struct_size_ratio (different-SIZED seeds); the iteration-depth subtype uses per-seed depth_reach; the decoded-codepoint subtype uses value_distance_reached sliding windows; and the NEW homogenization subtype uses corpus_size_ratio, a WHOLE-CORPUS composition comparison that needs NO byte-alignment, NO fixed offset, and NO per-branch >=3-seed floor (it scans the full on-disk queues per arm) -- so it is exactly the tool for the structural/derived gates whose per-branch seed-set tools returned no data.

corpus_size_ratio is branch-INDEPENDENT (memoized per (target, arm-pair)): one harfbuzz cmplog-vs-naive scan scores every harfbuzz branch routed to the homogenization hypothesis, so that hypothesis confirms a SHARED target-level mechanism for the group rather than discriminating the branches from one another. It needs on-disk corpora for BOTH arms (harfbuzz/openthread on s4).

On-disk corpus / cov-image dependence: operand_enrichment, value_distance_reached, depth_reach, corpus_size_ratio all need on-disk corpora or the target cov image. For this shape those exist for harfbuzz and openthread on s4. Cross-server: a branch is NOT marked decidable:false merely because its corpus is off-server -- the owning server arbitrates its own targets.

Seed floor: operand_enrichment / value_distance_reached / depth_reach need >=3 W and >=3 L seeds in db/blockers.sqlite. A no_gate_signature or null-metric return caused by <3 seeds is a DATA reason (treat as inconclusive), NOT confirmation of a no-operand or non-discriminable hypothesis. corpus_size_ratio does NOT have this floor (it scans the whole queue), which is why it can score the structural branches the seed-set tools could not.
notes:
ROUND-2 re-design (loop-until-dry). SUPERSET/FREEZE: all FIVE prior KEEP hypotheses are carried forward VERBATIM in rule and direction (i2s_decoy_substitution_target_depletion, i2s_disrupts_havoc_sequence_accumulation, i2s_overhead_on_derived_gate_no_operand, i2s_overhead_on_iteration_depth_gate, i2s_decoded_codepoint_byte_reachable_gate) so the 23 confirmed branches re-validate unchanged -- no rule edited, no relaxed-threshold re-test. The only changes to KEEP hypotheses are member_hint_branches bookkeeping (the depth and decoded-codepoint hints that did NOT validate in the campaign were emptied/moved; the rules are untouched).

PRIORS KEPT: 5 (all). The 6 STILL-UNLABELED branches (harfbuzz 5311/5723/5881/5931/5934/5954) each failed all their scored prior rules in the campaign -- not because the wrong rule was tried but because the per-branch seed-set tools (depth_reach for 5311/5931; value_distance_reached for 5954) ran and came out flat/below-threshold, or returned no data (only 3 of 5 rules scorable for five of the six -- the seed-set tools lacked the >=3-seed floor on these structural/derived gates). A relaxed-threshold re-test of those same rules is forbidden, so round-2 pivots to a genuinely different MEASUREMENT.

NEW MECHANISM (1, discriminable) -- i2s_homogenizes_cmplog_corpus (5311, 5931, 5934), via corpus_size_ratio (a BUILT registry tool NOT previously used on this shape; its i2s_homogenizes_corpus rule is already validated on harfbuzz for the i2s_vp_LWLW shape). The mechanism is GENUINELY DIFFERENT from every prior here: not a fixed-offset decoy (operand_enrichment), not a per-seed size or depth lift, but a WHOLE-CORPUS, branch-independent claim that I2S inflates AND homogenizes the cmplog corpus onto a few CMP-matched byte motifs, collapsing the byte-diversity the winner's Ragel/state-machine gates need. The cards directly support this for the three members: 5931's loser corpus is 'dominated by repeated CMP-derived literals (runs of f9, magic ASCII hasc/csah/SHOT)', 5934's blocking seeds are 'saturated with a repeating 72 10 00 / 8d 10 00 lexical pattern', and 5311's cmplog 'spends budget aligning comparison operands rather than growing buffer volume'. G1: a 'no corpus effect' alternative (the passive-overhead story already labeled by i2s_overhead_on_derived_gate_no_operand) predicts corpus_size_ratio ~ 1.0 AND composition_entropy_ratio ~ 1.0 and does NOT fire; only an active corpus-poisoning mechanism predicts inflation + entropy-collapse. G2: corpus_size_ratio reads only saved-corpus byte composition, never the resolve pattern. KEY: this tool is branch-independent, so it confirms a SHARED target-level homogenization mechanism for the harfbuzz group (it does not discriminate the three from each other) -- that is the honest granularity these operand-less structural gates admit.

ORCHESTRATOR WIRING (one line, no new tool code): bench/arbitrate.py drives corpus_size_ratio arms from CS_ARMS.get(shape); add  "i2s_vp_LW_W": ("cmplog", "naive")  to CS_ARMS so _cs_winner/_cs_loser resolve (analogous to the existing ctx_coverage_LW / i2s_vp_LWLW entries). Without this entry the homogenization rule is UNSCORABLE and those branches fall to fallback=inconclusive.

DECIDABLE:FALSE SUB-GROUP -- i2s_oversolve_postshaping_derived_predicate (5723, 5881, 5954). 5723 (cmplog satisfies the ZWJ+Extended_Pictographic TRUE conjunct) and 5881 (cmplog keeps producing Malayalam/Tamil -- a SPECIFIC script) are OVER-SOLVE losers: cmplog reaches MORE of a directed structure, the OPPOSITE of homogenization-starvation, so corpus_size_ratio's entropy-collapse signal would be a FALSE positive and cannot stand in for them. 5954 (width-2 decoded Thai codepoint) failed value_distance_reached in the campaign because cmplog also lands 0x0E-high byte-pairs (just the wrong low byte) -- a width-2 chance-alignment limit the registry itself flags -- and no other built tool discriminates it. Per G3 this is an honest non-discriminable group (decidable:false, rule:'false'); it would need a per-call-context / decoded-value-provenance tool or a width-aware byte-pair tool that is NOT in the registry. I did NOT invent a mechanism to force a label. round-2 reshuffle relative to round-1: 5934 MOVED OUT of decidable:false into i2s_homogenizes_cmplog_corpus (it shows the starvation/homogenization, not over-solve, signature); 5954 MOVED INTO decidable:false (its value-distance test refuted, no replacement tool).

DECISION ORDER: the 5 frozen priors interleaved with the new homogenization test, catch-all LAST. Specifically: decoy-substitution, sequence-accumulation, iteration-depth, decoded-codepoint, THEN i2s_homogenizes_cmplog_corpus, THEN the no-operand overhead catch-all. The homogenization test is placed AFTER the per-branch tools (so any branch a finer per-branch tool can label gets that more specific label first) but BEFORE the passive-overhead catch-all (so a branch whose cmplog corpus is actively homogenized is labeled as such rather than mislabeled as passive overhead). The decidable:false group has rule 'false', never matches, and falls to fallback=inconclusive.

OUTCOME SUMMARY: PRIORS KEPT 5 (all, verbatim rules). NEW MECHANISMS 1 discriminable (i2s_homogenizes_cmplog_corpus via corpus_size_ratio; covers 5311/5931/5934 as a shared target-level claim; needs the one-line CS_ARMS wiring). DECIDABLE:FALSE 1 sub-group of 3 branches (5723/5881 post-shaping over-solve + 5954 width-2 decoded-codepoint, no built discriminator).
